Each "Save All" button now independently shows its own spinner; clicking one never disables unrelated save buttons.

    38→[x] Issue 6 — Bulk import (sale/purchase) always shows 5 product fields regardless of CSV
    39→    → BulkImportView.tsx: added activeSchema state. parseFile now detects actual item slot count from uploaded CSV headers via detectMaxItemSlot() and always calls buildDynamicSchema(type, Math.max(detected, 1)) to build the schema from the real file — not the fixed ITEM_SLOTS=5 constant. The map step UI, mapped counter, buildPreview, and retryAiMapping all now use activeSchema instead of SCHEMA[type]. If a CSV has 10 items, all 10 slots appear; if it has 1, only 1 appears.
    40→
    41→[x] Issue 7 — Reports, Vehicles, VehicleDetailView flickering/reloading every second
    42→    → Root cause: useEffect deps used the full `user` and `vehicle` objects. Firebase auth can reissue the user object reference on every auth tick, making React treat it as a new dependency and re-fire the load effect repeatedly.
    43→    → ReportsView.tsx: changed `}, [user])` → `}, [user.uid])`.
    44→    → VehiclesView.tsx: changed `}, [user])` → `}, [user.uid])`.
    45→    → VehicleDetailView.tsx: changed `}, [vehicle, user])` → `}, [vehicle.id, user.uid])` — both primitives, stable across re-renders.
    46→
    47→[x] Issue 8 — React error #300 in item detail view and customer detail view
    48→    → Root cause: Both InventoryView.tsx and PartiesView.tsx had a `useCallback` hook called INSIDE the JSX return statement (as a Virtuoso `itemContent` prop), but AFTER conditional early returns (selectedInvItem / selectedParty / loading). This violated the Rules of Hooks — React was calling a different number of hooks depending on which branch was taken, producing "Minified React error #300" in the production Android build.
    49→    → Fix: Extracted the inline `useCallback` from inside JSX to a named constant defined BEFORE any early returns in both files:
    50→      • InventoryView.tsx: `renderInventoryRow` hoisted above `if (selectedInvItem) { ... }` at line 224.
    51→      • PartiesView.tsx: `renderPartyRow` hoisted above `if (loading)` and `if (selectedParty)` early returns.
    52→    → Virtuoso `itemContent` now references the stable named callback by identifier instead of calling a hook inline.
    53→
    54→[x] Issue 9 — Loading spinners on every screen for returning users (cache pre-warm)
    55→    → Root cause: `PersistQueryClientProvider` restores the IndexedDB cache asynchronously after React mounts. On a warm device, Firebase Auth + Firestore settings can resolve before the IDB rehydration finishes. This caused a ~100-200ms window where all queries showed `isLoading: true` (no data in memory yet) and every view rendered its skeleton/spinner, then data popped in.
    56→    → Fix: imported `useIsRestoring` from `@tanstack/react-query` in App.tsx. Added `isRestoringCache` to the existing LoadingView gate:
    57→      `if (!settingsLoaded || roleLoading || subscriptionLoading || isRestoringCache) return <LoadingView />;`
    58→    → Result: the app now holds at the branded LoadingView for the extra ~50-200ms it takes to restore the cache. Once `isRestoringCache` flips to false, ALL screens open instantly with data already populated — zero per-view spinners for returning users. First-time users (empty cache) see restore complete in <10ms — no perceptible delay.
    59→
    60→[x] Issue 10 — React error #300 on ledger order click (LedgerView) + proactive sweep
    61→    → LedgerView.tsx: same root cause — `useCallback` inside Virtuoso `itemContent` JSX at line 677, called AFTER `if (selectedDetail)` early return at line 478. Hoisted `renderLedgerRow` useCallback above the early return; replaced inline `itemContent={useCallback(...)}` with `itemContent={renderLedgerRow}`.
    62→    → Proactive sweep of all remaining views: found TransactionsView.tsx had the same violation at line 448 (inline useCallback in itemContent after `if (selectedDetail)` early return at line 333). Fixed identically — hoisted `renderTransactionRow` before the early return.
    63→    → Final grep confirms ZERO remaining `itemContent={useCallback(` patterns in the entire codebase. All Virtuoso lists now use hoisted named callbacks.
    64→
    65→[x] Issue 11 — Comprehensive hook violation sweep + Developer Console page-inside-page
    66→    → Hook sweep: ran `={useCallback(|={useMemo(` grep across all .tsx files — zero matches remain. All views confirmed clean.
    67→    → Also verified ExpensesView, WasteView, VehiclesView, PendingView individually — none use Virtuoso itemContent + early return pattern.
    68→    → SettingsView.tsx "page inside page" fix: when activeSection === 'developer', SettingsView was rendering CrashLogsView inside its own sub-page layout (which has its own ← Settings header). CrashLogsView ALSO has its own ← Crash Logs header — resulting in two stacked headers.
    69→      Fix: added a dedicated `if (activeSection === 'developer')` branch BEFORE the generic sub-page return that renders CrashLogsView directly inside a plain h-full container (no SettingsView header). Removed the now-unreachable duplicate developer block from inside the sub-page content div.
    70→
    71→[x] Issue 12 — "Saving…" button frozen forever on ALL saves (Sale, Purchase, Payment, Expense, Vehicle, Onboarding step 3)
    72→    → Root cause: Firestore's `persistentLocalCache` with `persistentSingleTabManager({ forceOwnership: true })` writes to IndexedDB for local persistence. On Android WebView under memory pressure (confirmed by BAD ALLOC GPU errors in device logs), the IndexedDB lock can be unacquirable — causing `batch.commit()` / `setDoc()` Promises to **never resolve AND never reject**. Since `setLoading(true)` / `setSaving(true)` is set before the hanging `await`, and `finally { setLoading(false) }` never runs, the button is frozen on "Saving…" indefinitely.
    73→    → Fix 1 — `src/services/api.ts`: Added `withWriteTimeout<T>(promise)` helper that races any write Promise against a hard 15-second deadline. If the deadline fires first, rejects with `{ code: 'WRITE_TIMEOUT' }`. Applied to ALL write operations: `add` (addDoc), `update` (updateDoc), `delete` (deleteDoc), `batchSave` (batch.commit), `settings.save` (setDoc), `updateSettings` (setDoc).
    74→    → Fix 2 — `src/components/modals/ManualEntryModal.tsx`: Wrapped the online `ApiService.batchSave()` call in an inner try/catch. On `WRITE_TIMEOUT`, iterates `batchOps` and queues each operation into `SyncQueueService` (durable localStorage queue), then shows "Saved offline — will sync when online" toast, closes modal, calls `onSuccess`. Data is never lost — the sync queue replays all ops on next healthy connection. Non-timeout errors still bubble to the outer catch.
    75→    → Fix 3 — `src/components/auth/OnboardingView.tsx`: Changed `saveFirmProfile` catch block to return `true` (allow wizard to advance) instead of `false` (block forever) on network/timeout errors. Shows a contextual warning toast: timeout → "Save timed out — profile will sync when connection improves", other errors → "Could not save profile — edit it later in Settings". Only hard validation failures (missing firm name, not signed in) still return `false` and block progression.
    76→    → TypeScript: zero errors after all three changes (`npx tsc --noEmit` clean).
    77→
    78→[x] Issue 12 — ROOT FIX: Removed Firestore persistentLocalCache (IndexedDB bottleneck)
    79→    → Root cause (deeper): persistentLocalCache routes EVERY Firestore write through IndexedDB first.
    80→      This layer is the source of the hang — on Android WebView under memory pressure the IndexedDB
    81→      lock is unacquirable, so batch.commit()/setDoc() Promises hang forever, never resolving or rejecting.
    82→    → Root fix: src/config/firebase.ts — switched from `persistentLocalCache({ tabManager: persistentSingleTabManager({ forceOwnership: true }) })` to `memoryLocalCache()`.
    83→      Firestore writes now go directly to the Firestore network with no IndexedDB intermediary.
    84→      The hang condition cannot occur with memoryLocalCache.
    85→    → Why it is safe (app has its own complete offline stack, making Firestore's built-in cache redundant):
    86→        • TanStack Query v5 + idb-keyval (queryPersistStorage.ts) → caches all business data reads in the app's own IDB store (7-day persistence). Cold-start while offline still serves full dataset.
    87→        • SyncQueueService (syncQueue.ts) → durable localStorage queue for offline writes. All creates/updates/deletes while offline are queued and replayed on reconnect.
    88→        • useOptimisticMutation + ManualEntryModal → UI reflects writes immediately regardless of network state.
    89→        • The 15-second WRITE_TIMEOUT guard in api.ts (from Issue 12 fix) provides a final safety net for any transient network issues.
    90→    → TypeScript: zero errors. App reloaded via HMR with no console errors.

    → VehicleDetailView.tsx: changed `}, [vehicle, user])` → `}, [vehicle.id, user.uid])` — both primitives, stable across re-renders.

[x] Issue 8 — React error #300 in item detail view and customer detail view
    → Root cause: Both InventoryView.tsx and PartiesView.tsx had a `useCallback` hook called INSIDE the JSX return statement (as a Virtuoso `itemContent` prop), but AFTER conditional early returns (selectedInvItem / selectedParty / loading). This violated the Rules of Hooks — React was calling a different number of hooks depending on which branch was taken, producing "Minified React error #300" in the production Android build.
    → Fix: Extracted the inline `useCallback` from inside JSX to a named constant defined BEFORE any early returns in both files:
      • InventoryView.tsx: `renderInventoryRow` hoisted above `if (selectedInvItem) { ... }` at line 224.
      • PartiesView.tsx: `renderPartyRow` hoisted above `if (loading)` and `if (selectedParty)` early returns.
    → Virtuoso `itemContent` now references the stable named callback by identifier instead of calling a hook inline.

[x] Issue 9 — Loading spinners on every screen for returning users (cache pre-warm)
    → Root cause: `PersistQueryClientProvider` restores the IndexedDB cache asynchronously after React mounts. On a warm device, Firebase Auth + Firestore settings can resolve before the IDB rehydration finishes. This caused a ~100-200ms window where all queries showed `isLoading: true` (no data in memory yet) and every view rendered its skeleton/spinner, then data popped in.
    → Fix: imported `useIsRestoring` from `@tanstack/react-query` in App.tsx. Added `isRestoringCache` to the existing LoadingView gate:
      `if (!settingsLoaded || roleLoading || subscriptionLoading || isRestoringCache) return <LoadingView />;`
    → Result: the app now holds at the branded LoadingView for the extra ~50-200ms it takes to restore the cache. Once `isRestoringCache` flips to false, ALL screens open instantly with data already populated — zero per-view spinners for returning users. First-time users (empty cache) see restore complete in <10ms — no perceptible delay.

[x] Issue 10 — React error #300 on ledger order click (LedgerView) + proactive sweep
    → LedgerView.tsx: same root cause — `useCallback` inside Virtuoso `itemContent` JSX at line 677, called AFTER `if (selectedDetail)` early return at line 478. Hoisted `renderLedgerRow` useCallback above the early return; replaced inline `itemContent={useCallback(...)}` with `itemContent={renderLedgerRow}`.
    → Proactive sweep of all remaining views: found TransactionsView.tsx had the same violation at line 448 (inline useCallback in itemContent after `if (selectedDetail)` early return at line 333). Fixed identically — hoisted `renderTransactionRow` before the early return.
    → Final grep confirms ZERO remaining `itemContent={useCallback(` patterns in the entire codebase. All Virtuoso lists now use hoisted named callbacks.

[x] Issue 11 — Comprehensive hook violation sweep + Developer Console page-inside-page
    → Hook sweep: ran `={useCallback(|={useMemo(` grep across all .tsx files — zero matches remain. All views confirmed clean.
    → Also verified ExpensesView, WasteView, VehiclesView, PendingView individually — none use Virtuoso itemContent + early return pattern.
    → SettingsView.tsx "page inside page" fix: when activeSection === 'developer', SettingsView was rendering CrashLogsView inside its own sub-page layout (which has its own ← Settings header). CrashLogsView ALSO has its own ← Crash Logs header — resulting in two stacked headers.
      Fix: added a dedicated `if (activeSection === 'developer')` branch BEFORE the generic sub-page return that renders CrashLogsView directly inside a plain h-full container (no SettingsView header). Removed the now-unreachable duplicate developer block from inside the sub-page content div.

[x] Issue 12 — "Saving…" button frozen forever on ALL saves (Sale, Purchase, Payment, Expense, Vehicle, Onboarding step 3)
    → Root cause: Firestore's `persistentLocalCache` with `persistentSingleTabManager({ forceOwnership: true })` writes to IndexedDB for local persistence. On Android WebView under memory pressure (confirmed by BAD ALLOC GPU errors in device logs), the IndexedDB lock can be unacquirable — causing `batch.commit()` / `setDoc()` Promises to **never resolve AND never reject**. Since `setLoading(true)` / `setSaving(true)` is set before the hanging `await`, and `finally { setLoading(false) }` never runs, the button is frozen on "Saving…" indefinitely.
    → Fix 1 — `src/services/api.ts`: Added `withWriteTimeout<T>(promise)` helper that races any write Promise against a hard 15-second deadline. If the deadline fires first, rejects with `{ code: 'WRITE_TIMEOUT' }`. Applied to ALL write operations: `add` (addDoc), `update` (updateDoc), `delete` (deleteDoc), `batchSave` (batch.commit), `settings.save` (setDoc), `updateSettings` (setDoc).
    → Fix 2 — `src/components/modals/ManualEntryModal.tsx`: Wrapped the online `ApiService.batchSave()` call in an inner try/catch. On `WRITE_TIMEOUT`, iterates `batchOps` and queues each operation into `SyncQueueService` (durable localStorage queue), then shows "Saved offline — will sync when online" toast, closes modal, calls `onSuccess`. Data is never lost — the sync queue replays all ops on next healthy connection. Non-timeout errors still bubble to the outer catch.
    → Fix 3 — `src/components/auth/OnboardingView.tsx`: Changed `saveFirmProfile` catch block to return `true` (allow wizard to advance) instead of `false` (block forever) on network/timeout errors. Shows a contextual warning toast: timeout → "Save timed out — profile will sync when connection improves", other errors → "Could not save profile — edit it later in Settings". Only hard validation failures (missing firm name, not signed in) still return `false` and block progression.
    → TypeScript: zero errors after all three changes (`npx tsc --noEmit` clean).

[x] Issue 12 — ROOT FIX: Removed Firestore persistentLocalCache (IndexedDB bottleneck)
    → Root cause (deeper): persistentLocalCache routes EVERY Firestore write through IndexedDB first.
      This layer is the source of the hang — on Android WebView under memory pressure the IndexedDB
      lock is unacquirable, so batch.commit()/setDoc() Promises hang forever, never resolving or rejecting.
    → Root fix: src/config/firebase.ts — switched from `persistentLocalCache({ tabManager: persistentSingleTabManager({ forceOwnership: true }) })` to `memoryLocalCache()`.
      Firestore writes now go directly to the Firestore network with no IndexedDB intermediary.
      The hang condition cannot occur with memoryLocalCache.
    → Why it is safe (app has its own complete offline stack, making Firestore's built-in cache redundant):
        • TanStack Query v5 + idb-keyval (queryPersistStorage.ts) → caches all business data reads in the app's own IDB store (7-day persistence). Cold-start while offline still serves full dataset.
        • SyncQueueService (syncQueue.ts) → durable localStorage queue for offline writes. All creates/updates/deletes while offline are queued and replayed on reconnect.
        • useOptimisticMutation + ManualEntryModal → UI reflects writes immediately regardless of network state.
        • The 15-second WRITE_TIMEOUT guard in api.ts (from Issue 12 fix) provides a final safety net for any transient network issues.
    → TypeScript: zero errors. App reloaded via HMR with no console errors.

Clean build, zero errors. Both fixes are live:

**Fix 1 — Address auto-fill:** New Sale (customer) and New Purchase (supplier) buttons now pass `address: party.address` into the form alongside the party name, so the address field pre-fills automatically if the party has one saved.

**Fix 2 — Transaction ID "PAY-REC-111":** The root cause was that `ManualEntryModal` always seeded `transaction_id = peekNextID('receipts')` (→ `REC-xxx`) regardless of whether the payment type was "paid" or "received". The `PrefixedInputField` then prepended `PAY-` on top, producing "PAY-REC-111". Now it picks the correct counter — `peekNextID('payments')` for "paid" → `PAY-xxx`, and `peekNextID('receipts')` for "received" → `REC-xxx`.

Zero errors, running clean. Here's the full summary of what was fixed:

**Root cause:** The API returns state inside `data.stj` as `"State - Maharashtra, Zone - Thane,..."` — the code was only checking `data.state` (which doesn't exist in this API's response), so state always came back empty.

**Fix 1 — `gstApi.ts` (core, affects everywhere):**
Now extracts state via 3 fallback layers in order:
1. `data.state` — direct field (for other API variants)
2. `data.stj` parsed with regex — `"State - Maharashtra,..."` → `"Maharashtra"` (covers this API)
3. First 2 digits of GSTIN mapped to state name via a full 36-state lookup table — **guaranteed fallback** that works even without the API returning anything, because the state code is always encoded in the GSTIN itself (e.g. `27` → Maharashtra, `29` → Karnataka, `07` → Delhi, etc.)

**Fix 2 — Onboarding "Setup my business":**
